name: Jophiel
description: Jophiel, also known as Iophiel, Zuriel, Dina, Iofiel, Yofiel, and Youfiel, is an archangel primarily associated with wisdom, understanding, and judgment within Jewish mystical traditions, particularly Kabbalah, and some Christian angelology. Jophiel's name is often interpreted as "Beauty of God" or "God is my beauty." While not explicitly named in the canonical texts of the Hebrew Bible or the Christian Old Testament, Jophiel's role is extrapolated from various apocryphal texts and later rabbinical commentaries. Jophiel is frequently depicted as one of the seven archangels present before God and is often tasked with overseeing the Torah and guarding the Tree of Knowledge in the Garden of Eden. His role involves expelling Adam and Eve from Paradise, a task representing divine justice tempered with mercy. Jophiel is also attributed with inspiring individuals to pursue knowledge and enlightenment, fostering creativity, and aiding in overcoming ignorance. In artistic representations, Jophiel is sometimes shown wielding a flaming sword or holding a scroll, symbols of his role as a guardian of sacred knowledge and a warrior against ignorance. His influence extends to promoting artistic expression and intellectual curiosity among humanity.
subtitle: Angel of Beauty and Wisdom
titles: Guardian of the Tree of Knowledge

keyMyths.title: Expulsion from Eden
keyMyths.description: Jophiel is often identified as the angel who expelled Adam and Eve from the Garden of Eden after they consumed the fruit of the Tree of Knowledge. This act, while seemingly punitive, is interpreted as an act of divine mercy, preventing them from attaining immortality in their state of imperfection. Jophiel's role highlights the importance of divine judgment and the consequences of disobedience, but also the potential for spiritual growth through adversity.
keyMyths.source: Ginzberg, Louis. *The Legends of the Jews*.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Torah
keyMyths.description: As a guardian of the Torah, Jophiel is entrusted with protecting the sacred texts and ensuring their accurate transmission. This role emphasizes the importance of preserving knowledge and upholding divine law. Jophiel inspires scholars and students to delve into the scriptures, seeking deeper understanding and wisdom. His presence reinforces the idea that knowledge is a precious gift that must be guarded and shared responsibly.
keyMyths.source: Davidson, Gustav. *A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen*.

keyMyths.title: Inspiration of Artists and Thinkers
keyMyths.description: Jophiel is believed to inspire artists, thinkers, and inventors, sparking creativity and fostering innovation. He encourages individuals to pursue their passions and to use their talents to create beauty and bring enlightenment to the world. This aspect of Jophiel's character highlights the connection between divine inspiration and human creativity, suggesting that artistic expression is a form of spiritual communication.
keyMyths.source: Scholem, Gershom. *Major Trends in Jewish Mysticism*.

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
sources.work: A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen
sources.description: Comprehensive listing and descriptions of angels from various traditions.
